name: Quick Explorer

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Support attestations in build
This change is just to get Obsidian's community site to give QE a decent score on some things.  (Though it's still reporting bogus typing errors.)  If you're just *using* the plugin, there are no real changes between 0.2.17 and 0.2.18.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Add emacs-style menu keys (fix #123)

- Mod-n/p/f/b now move through menus
- Some quick lint fixes for Obsidian's new review process

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fix compatibility issues w/Obsidian 1.11+

Page preview functionality should now be restored.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved move, rename, and folder-create workflows

- Support creating folders w/out file explorer (#113), allowing
  folders to be created even if the files plugin is disabled
  or hidden. Newly created folders are immediately shown in
  the current Quick Explorer.
- Moving a file or folder no longer requires the files plugin to
  be active, and after the move, Quick Explorer navigates to
  the file or folder's new location
- Renaming a file or folder also returns to the Quick Explorer
  menu, instead of closing after the operation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improve context menus and link opening by keyboard

- Made context menu items more closely match groups/ordering of
  the File Explorer context menu
- Support opening files to the right or in a new window via
  Mod-Alt-Enter and Mod-Alt-Shift-Enter (as well as clicks,
  which already worked)
- Moved "Show in Quick Explorer" to the "system" menu group in
  other context menus, where it will be grouped with "Reveal file
  in navigation", "Show in system explorer", etc.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Keep hover editors from closing when mouse moves

If the mouse recently opened a hover editor and you left it there, Quick Explorer would open hover previews and then immediately close them.  This version fixes that issue and generally prevents mouse movements from affecting hover editors opened by Quick Explorer.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fix keyboard selection jitter during hover

Previously, if you used arrows or home/end to navigate a menu/folder that was taller than the available space, and the mouse was over any part of the menu, the selection would jump to the item at the mouse position (because the browser issues a new mouseover event when the items scroll up or down).

This release fixes that issue by ignoring mouseover events when the mouse hasn't moved relative to the screen.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: ### Don't include file counts in search (Fix #63)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Support 0.16.3 breadcrumbs and allow hiding

If you have Obsidian 0.16.3 or above, you can now click its tab title bar "breadcrumbs" to access Quick Explorer (or double-click to open the folder in the built-in File Explorer).

There is now also a [Style Settings](https://github.com/mgmeyers/obsidian-style-settings) option you can use to hide the Quick Explorer, such that it does not show in the title bar or status bar.  If hidden, Quick Explorer keyboard commands will use the most recently active tab's header breadcrumbs to display its menus (assuming you're on 0.16.3 and the tab title bar is visible).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New feature: Navigation hotkeys!

Want to navigate through a lot of chronologically or numerically-sorted notes, as if they were a sort of virtual notebook?  (e.g. daily notes, weekly notes, Zettels, chapters, etc.)

Now you can easily step through all the files in a folder without even opening Quick Explorer: just use the new commands for "go next" and "go previous" to step to the next or previous file in the same folder, sorted using QE's sort order (folder note first, then subfolders, then local files).  (There are also "go first" and "go last" commands.)

Just add hotkeys and go!

(Also, this version contains a CSS compatibility fix for the Minimal theme!)

releases.description:
## Experimental multi-window support for 0.15.x

NOTE: due to a bug in Obsidian 0.15.1, page previews do not work in any window unless you use Hover Editor (which only works in the main window).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix #35: recursion error without Hover Editor

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
#### More Hover Editor features (arrow in, anti-pin)

Enhancements for the [Hover Editor plugin](https://github.com/nothingislost/obsidian-hover-editor/):

* Arrow right on a file to focus and pin a Hover Editor
* Auto-pinning of Quick Explorer-triggered popups is disabled, so you don't end up with dozens of bogus popups sticking around.  (But autopin will still work for mouse-triggered popups.)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improved compatibility w/Hover Editor plugin

Ensure that stray popovers are closed even if the mouse is over them, and that a new popover is opened when a different item is selected (even if the mouse isn't moved).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Misc. improvements:

* Fixed some keystrokes passing through to Live Preview
* Turn page preview on by default
* Remove confusing `..` folders
* Always show folder note at top of list

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
* Escape now closes the entire quick explorer (use <- arrow to go up a level)
* Backslash opens context menu
* Right clicking a parent menu item doesn't end up with double selections
* Closing a context menu restores the preview view, if active
* Child menus nest closer together to use up less screen width
* A "Show in Quick Explorer" menu item now appears in file menus
releases.name: 0.1.2 - Misc. menu improvements, add \ key
